title_en: The Savannah Bananas Bring Back a Negro Leagues Team
title_zh: 萨凡纳香蕉队重振一支黑人联赛球队
rubric_en: The organization is best known for its dance numbers and trick plays. Now it’s reviving one of the most entertaining—and controversial—franchises in baseball history.
rubric_zh: 这个组织最出名的是热闹的舞蹈秀和花哨的战术配合，如今它正在复兴棒球史上最有看点、也最具争议的球队之一。

paragraphs.en: “My dad was a big Lakers fan,” Kobe Shaquille Robinson told me, indulging an admittedly obvious question. Robinson was born in 2001, in the middle of Kobe Bryant and Shaquille O’Neal’s three-NBA-championship run. But he discovered early on that his name couldn’t help him shoot a basketball. As an athlete, he stood out on the pitcher’s mound.
paragraphs.zh: “我爸是个很资深的湖人球迷，”科比·沙奎尔·罗宾森对我说，顺着一个说起来相当明显的问题展开。罗宾森出生于2001年，正值科比·布莱恩特和沙奎尔·奥尼尔完成三次NBA总冠军的巅峰期。然而他很早就意识到，自己的名字并不能帮他投进篮球。作为一名运动员，他在投手丘上很有天赋。

paragraphs.en: Robinson is 6 foot 2 and lanky; when we met, he was wearing his hair in two-strand twists. We were talking on a Saturday afternoon in Memphis, in a retro-style downtown stadium named after an auto-parts chain. It was, in a way, the perfect venue for a conversation with an up-and-coming ballplayer—a minor-league park with all the trimmings of a major-league one. It was also, objectively speaking, an unusual workplace for a Black athlete in 2026.
paragraphs.zh: 罗宾森身高6英尺2英寸，个子修长；我们见面时，他把头发梳成了两股辫。那是一个周六下午，我们在孟菲斯的一座以一家汽车零部件连锁店命名的复古风市中心球场交谈。它在某种程度上，是与一位正崛起的球员对话的完美场地——一座拥有大联盟球场所有配套设施的次级联盟球场。客观来说，这也是一处在2026年对一名黑人运动员来说颇为不同寻常的“工作场所”。

paragraphs.en: Back in the mid-1980s, during the prime of Ozzie Smith, Rickey Henderson, Tony Gwynn, and Dwight Gooden, more than 18 percent of Major League Baseball players were Black. Now that figure is just below 7 percent—right around where it was in 1956, less than a decade after Jackie Robinson broke the color line.
paragraphs.zh: 回到1980年代中期，奥齐·史密斯、里基·亨德森、托尼·格温和德怀特·古丁当红时，大联盟球员中黑人占比超过18%。如今，这一数字刚刚低于7%，大致回到1956年的水平——那是杰基·罗宾逊打破种族隔离后不到十年。

paragraphs.en: No single reason explains Black Americans’ diminished footprint in the sport; the high cost of equipment and travel ball, dwindling municipal funding for youth leagues, the rise of the NFL and the NBA, and a parallel surge of Latino talent have all contributed. Despite these factors, Kobe Robinson still dreamed of a life in baseball. “I just felt like the man out there,” he said. “So I stuck with it.”
paragraphs.zh: 并不存在单一原因能解释黑人美国人在这项运动中所占比重下降；装备和外出比赛开销高、青年联赛市政经费减少、NFL和NBA崛起，以及拉美裔人才的同步激增，都在其中发挥了作用。尽管有这些因素，科比·罗宾森仍然梦想在棒球界有所作为。“我只觉得自己就是那位站在场上的男人，”他说。“所以我坚持了下来。”

paragraphs.en: Robinson’s fastball, which earned him the nickname “Hot Sauce,” carried him from a Tennessee community college to the 2021 MLB draft, where he was selected by the San Diego Padres. Injuries, however, stymied his early career: He had issues with his elbow, then his shoulder. In 2024, the Padres released him. The closest he ever got to the big show was A-ball, three rungs below the majors.
paragraphs.zh: 罗宾逊的快速球让他赢得了“热酱”这个绰号，并从田纳西州的一所社区学院一路闯入2021年MLB选秀，在那里他被圣地亚哥教士队选中。然而，伤病打断了他早期职业生涯：先是肘部出问题，后来又是肩膀。2024年，教士队将他释放。离开大联盟最近的一次，不过是到“小联盟”，也就是距离大联盟还差三档的等级。

paragraphs.en: At 23, Robinson was out of baseball and, he said, “in a dark space.” He took overnight caregiving shifts at a group home, delivered packages for Amazon, and searched for a way to get back on the field. Last fall, after a year on the sidelines, he found a potential opening: The Savannah Bananas were hiring.
paragraphs.zh: 23岁那年，罗宾逊脱离了棒球生涯，按他的说法，那时他“陷入了黑暗期”。他在一处寄宿之家值夜班照护，给亚马逊送快递，并一直在寻找重返球场的机会。去年秋天，在离队一年后，他找到了一个可能的机会：萨凡纳香蕉队正在招人。

paragraphs.en: Over the past three-plus years, the Bananas have gone from a baseball curiosity to a cultural juggernaut. The team tours the country playing what it calls Banana Ball: a family-friendly, souped-up, TikTok-ready version of the national pastime. Games feature singing and dancing and celebrity cameos, plus backflipping outfielders, stilt-walking batters, and the occasional double to the gap. Last year, according to the organization’s own data, the Bananas and their affiliated teams sold 2.2 million tickets—more than 11 different MLB franchises.
paragraphs.zh: 在过去三年多里，香蕉队已从一个棒球奇观转变为文化现象级团队。该队在全国巡回比赛，开展他们称之为“香蕉球”的赛事：这是一个面向家庭、升级版、适合TikTok传播的版本，属于美国职业棒球这项国球。比赛里有唱歌和舞蹈、有名人客串，还会有能做后空翻的外野手、踩高跷打者，以及偶尔出现的“空档处双杀球”。去年，根据该组织自己的数据，香蕉队及其关联队伍售出票数达220万张，超过了11支不同的MLB特许球队。

paragraphs.en: The Bananas are frequently compared to the Harlem Globetrotters. But unlike their basketball counterparts, who ritually defeat the rival Washington Generals, the Bananas don’t script the outcomes of their games. They play against—and sometimes lose to—a rotating band of teams with their own personalities and followings. Among their opponents are the denim-clad Texas Tailgaters, the often-shirtless Party Animals, and the Firefighters, who make their entrance in full firefighting uniforms, as if to douse an inferno in right field.
paragraphs.zh: 人们常把香蕉队与哈莱姆格林博特球员相比。然而不同于那支篮球球队会“仪式性”击败对手华盛顿钢人队，香蕉队不预设比赛结果。它们会和一批拥有不同性格与球迷基础、会轮换上场的球队对阵，并有时会输掉比赛。其对手包括穿牛仔布的德州后场球迷后援队“Texas Tailgaters”、常年几乎不穿上衣的“Party Animals”，以及“Firefighters”——他们会穿着完整消防制服登场，仿佛要去右外场扑灭一场大火。

paragraphs.en: Robinson filled out a Prospective Banana Ball Player form and got invited to audition for a roster spot. He knew from a former teammate who played for the Firefighters that this would not be a traditional tryout. “I didn’t want to go dressed as just a baseball player, because that’s not what they look for,” he said. Instead, he went as Frozone, the Incredibles character voiced by Samuel L. Jackson. “It looked kind of goofy,” he said of his blue-and-white bodysuit. “But I said, I don’t care. I’m going out there, and I’m pitching.” In this context, pitching meant doing a synchronized twirl with his infielders, then firing a fastball across home plate.
paragraphs.zh: 罗宾逊填写了一份“未来香蕉球球员”申请表，并受邀去试训争取名单名额。他从曾在“消防队”效力过的前队友那里得知，这次试训并不传统。“我不想穿得像个普通的棒球运动员，因为他们看重的不是那样，”他回忆道。“于是我穿着《超人总动员》里由塞缪尔·L·杰克逊配音的角色弗罗森（Frozone）的蓝白色紧身连体服去的。”他又说：“看起来有点像搞笑角色。”“但我想，没关系。我走上场就行，我要‘投球’了。”在这个语境里，‘投球’意味着先和内野手做一个同步转体，再把速球掷向本垒板。

paragraphs.en: The scouts liked what they saw. A month later, Robinson was drafted by one of two expansion teams making their Banana Ball debut in 2026: the Indianapolis Clowns.
paragraphs.zh: 球探们看上了他的表现。一个月后，罗宾逊被两支将在2026年进行香蕉球首秀的扩军球队之一——印第安纳波利斯小丑队（Indianapolis Clowns）——选中。

paragraphs.en: Kobe Robinson was drafted by the San Diego Padres, but never made it past A-ball. The Indianapolis Clowns are a second chance at professional baseball. (Kevin Wurm for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 科比·罗宾逊曾被圣地亚哥教士队（San Diego Padres）选中，但从未走出A级球场。印第安纳波利斯小丑队给了他一个重返职业棒球的第二次机会。（Kevin Wurm for The Atlantic）

paragraphs.en: Unlike the other teams in the extended Bananas universe, the Clowns are not an original creation. They were a real baseball franchise that competed in the Negro Leagues; in 1952, they signed a teenage prospect named Hank Aaron. Like the Bananas, they were also an entertainment act. The Clowns traveled with acrobats, a “one-man jazz band” called Boogie Woogie Paul, and an actual circus clown. Some of the only existing footage of the original Clowns shows the long-limbed first baseman Reece “Goose” Tatum, who also played for the Globetrotters, dropping to his knees as if to pray for a base hit and getting awakened from a fainting spell by a smelly foot.
paragraphs.zh: 与香蕉球宇宙中的其他球队不同，小丑队并非原创球队。它是一支真实存在、曾在黑人联盟效力的棒球队；1952年，他们签下了名叫汉克·阿伦的少年新秀。与香蕉队一样，小丑队也曾是一支兼具娱乐属性的球队。球队巡演时随行的有特技演员、“单人爵士乐队”博基·乌吉·保罗（Boogie Woogie Paul）以及一位真正的小丑。仅存的少数小丑队录像里，可以看到长肢体的一垒手里斯·“鹅”·塔顿（Reece “Goose” Tatum）——他也曾效力于华莱士队（Globetrotters）——跪到地上仿佛在祈祷自己能打出安打，然后被一只散发异味的鞋子上的“脚气味”惊醒。

paragraphs.en: Jesse Cole, the 42-year-old impresario behind the Bananas, has said that relaunching the Clowns is a way to honor one of Banana Ball’s forebears and preserve the legacy of the Negro Leagues. Robinson was thrilled. The Clowns “paved this way for us,” he told me. “Now we have to bring it back to this day and age and make it even better.” The Clowns also provide an opportunity to increase Black representation in baseball. Robinson, who feared that his career was over, now has another shot.
paragraphs.zh: 萨凡纳香蕉队背后的42岁创业策划人杰西·柯尔表示，重启小丑队是为了向“香蕉球”先驱致敬，并延续黑人联赛的传承。罗宾逊对此感到非常兴奋。小丑队“给我们铺好了这条路，”他对我说。“现在我们必须把它带入当下的时代，并把它做得更好。”小丑队也提供了一个机会，可以提升棒球中的黑人代表性。曾经担心自己职业生涯已到尽头的罗宾逊，如今又有了新的机会。

paragraphs.en: Read: How the Negro Leagues shaped modern baseball
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：黑人联赛如何塑造了现代棒球

paragraphs.en: But the decision to revive the Indianapolis Clowns isn’t as straightforward as it may seem. Although the team’s antics were widely popular, they could also descend into racial caricature. The Clowns rankled both their Negro Leagues peers and Black sportswriters, chief among them Wendell Smith. The influential Pittsburgh Courier columnist called the team a “fourth-rate ‘Uncle Tom’ minstrel show.” He also accused the team’s white owner—who promoted one of his star pitchers as baseball’s version of the shuffling, feebleminded minstrel character Stepin Fetchit—of profiting from “the kind of nonsense which many white people like to believe is typical and characteristic of all Negroes.”
paragraphs.zh: 但复兴印第安纳波利斯小丑队的决定并不像看上去那么简单。尽管这支球队的闹剧式表演广受欢迎，它也曾滑向种族化的夸张讽刺。小丑队不仅让同为黑人联赛的球队不满，也惹恼了黑人体育记者，其中最重要的是温德尔·史密斯。这位《匹兹堡水手报》影响力很大的专栏作家称该队是“低档次的‘汤姆叔叔’式闹剧表演”。他还指责球队白人老板——这位老板把一位当红先发投手包装成棒球版“边走边抖、反应迟钝”的闹剧人物斯蒂平·费奇的化身——在“白人普遍喜欢相信的、并认为典型且代表所有黑人”的那种胡闹中赚取了钱。

paragraphs.en: The Savannah Bananas have risen to prominence by ostentatiously breaking the rules. Cole, who is white, often talks of his admiration for P. T. Barnum, the brash showman who would do anything to attract an audience. The Bananas’ owner rightly intuited that baseball, the most hidebound of American sports, didn’t know how to market itself to a new, social-media-enabled generation. Cole makes all of his public appearances in a banana-yellow tuxedo and banana-yellow top hat; he has made swaggering nonconformity part of the brand. “If you’re not getting criticized,” he has said, “you’re playing it too safe.”
paragraphs.zh: 萨凡纳香蕉队正是凭借对规则的张扬式突破而崛起。白人出身的科尔常谈到他对P·T·巴纳姆的钦佩——那位放荡张扬、为吸引观众不惜一切手段的演艺经纪人。香蕉队老板敏锐地意识到，作为美国最保守的运动之一，棒球并不懂得如何向新时代、社交媒体时代的观众推销自己。科尔每次公开露面都穿香蕉黄燕尾服和香蕉黄高顶礼帽；他把“带着一股自负的叛逆”变成了品牌的一部分。他说过一句话：“如果你没有被批评，那你就太保守了。”

paragraphs.en: Resurrecting the Clowns definitely isn’t playing it safe.
paragraphs.zh: 复兴“小丑队”绝对算不上循规蹈矩。

paragraphs.en: When Bob Kendrick saw Banana Ball for the first time, in 2022, he felt like he was watching something at once new and very familiar. The action on the field was fast-paced and bold, and the fans were rapt. For Kendrick, the president of the Negro Leagues Baseball Museum, in Kansas City, Missouri, the scene evoked the audacity of preintegration Black baseball—the daring to build something new in opposition to the mainstream.
paragraphs.zh: 鲍勃·肯德里克（Bob Kendrick）在2022年第一次看到“香蕉球”时，感受到的既陌生又熟悉。场上节奏快速、动作大胆，观众也被完全抓住。作为密苏里州堪萨斯城黑人联盟棒球博物馆馆长的肯德里克先生，对他来说，这一幕让人想起了种族隔离前黑人棒球的胆识——在主流体系之外敢于开创新事物。

paragraphs.en: That May, Kendrick gave Cole and his players a private tour of the museum as part of an ESPN documentary series on the Bananas. Kendrick took the team to the section that featured the Clowns and told Cole that he saw them as an ancestor of the Bananas. “I think that’s when he had the epiphany that someday he would bring back the Indianapolis Clowns,” Kendrick told me.
paragraphs.zh: 那年5月，肯德里克在一部关于香蕉队的ESPN纪录片系列拍摄期间，带着科尔和他的球员参观了博物馆，并给他们安排了私人导览。他把球队带到展示“小丑队”的展区，并告诉科尔，他认为香蕉队可以追溯到“小丑队”。肯德里克告诉我：“我想，就是在那时，他忽然醒悟，某一天他会把印第安纳波利斯小丑队（Indianapolis Clowns）带回。”

paragraphs.en: The Clowns’ revival is a business arrangement: The Negro Leagues museum, which owns the Clowns’ intellectual property, received a fee from the Bananas for the rights to use the team’s name and develop a set of new logos. The partnership, Kendrick said, “comes along at a perfect time,” as he’s raising $50 million to build a 35,000-square-foot museum campus in Kansas City. Beyond the cash infusion, the Bananas will bring attention to the Negro Leagues, putting Black baseball history in front of a potentially huge audience.
paragraphs.zh: “小丑队”的复兴是一项商业合作：黑人联盟博物馆拥有“小丑队”的知识产权，因此从香蕉队那里获得了一笔许可费用，用于使用球队名称并开发一套全新标识。肯德里克说，这一合作“来得非常及时”，因为他正在筹集5000万美元，在堪萨斯城建设一处占地35,000平方英尺的博物馆校园。除资金注入外，香蕉队还将为黑人联盟带来更多关注，让更广大的观众群体看到黑人棒球的历史。

paragraphs.en: From the January 2025 issue: Caleb Gayle on the return of America’s oldest Black rodeo
paragraphs.zh: 选自2025年1月号：Caleb Gayle 论美国最古老的黑人牛仔竞技（Black rodeo）回归一事。

paragraphs.en: In a video announcing the Clowns’ return, Kendrick offered a brief lesson on why the team mattered. He name-checked the luminaries who wore a Clowns uniform: an 18-year-old Hank Aaron, a 60-something Satchel Paige, and the pathbreaking Toni Stone, who became the first woman to get consistent playing time for a professional baseball club when she joined the Clowns in 1953. Kendrick explained that the team helped popularize “shadow ball,” an elaborate routine in which real and invisible baseballs are tossed around the infield. And he said that, until the Clowns folded in the late 1980s, they were the last team standing from the Negro Leagues.
paragraphs.zh: 在一段宣布“小丑队”重返赛场的视频中，Kendrick 简要解释了这支球队为何重要。他点名提及了穿过小丑队球衣的知名人物：18岁的汉克·阿伦、60多岁的萨奇·佩奇，以及开创先河的托尼·斯通——她在1953年加入小丑队后，成为首位在职业棒球俱乐部中稳定登场的女性。Kendrick 还解释说，这支球队帮助普及了“影子球（shadow ball）”这套复杂节目：在内场里抛出真假并存的棒球进行表演。他指出，在小丑队于20世纪80年代后期解散之前，它们一直是黑人联盟中最后一支仍在运作的球队。

paragraphs.en: The Indianapolis Clowns manager Buster Haywood and the player Toni Stone circa 1953 (Transcendental Graphics / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 1953年前后，印第安纳波利斯小丑队经理 Buster Haywood 与球员托尼·斯通（Transcendental Graphics / Getty）

paragraphs.en: Everything Kendrick said in the video is true. But that introduction to the Clowns is incomplete. Their full story is one of ingenuity and endurance, but also exploitation.
paragraphs.zh: Kendrick 在视频中说的每一句都是真的。但对小丑队的介绍并不完整。它的完整故事既充满了机智与韧性，也包含了剥削。

paragraphs.en: The history of racial caricature in baseball goes back nearly as far as the organized game itself. As early as the 1870s, blackface performers began, in the words of the historian James E. Brunson III, to “exploit the game’s lucrative possibilities.” Many decades later, a team called the Zulu Cannibal Giants pushed this minstrel tradition to a dreadful extreme, taking the field in bare feet and grass skirts with “war paint” slathered on their bodies.
paragraphs.zh: 美国棒球史上的种族化讽喻可以追溯到有组织比赛诞生的最初时期。早在1870年代，历史学家詹姆斯·E·布伦森三世称，戴黑脸（黑面具）表演者便开始“利用这项运动的利润丰厚前景”。许多年后，一支名为“祖鲁食人族巨人队”的球队把这种滑稽剧传统推向了令人不安的极端：他们赤脚上场，穿着草裙，并在身体上抹上“战斗用彩绘”。

paragraphs.en: The man who created the Cannibal Giants in the 1930s was a former Negro Leagues pitcher. The team’s Northeast booking agent was a New Yorker with a background in vaudeville. His name was Syd Pollock, and he would become best known for owning another barnstorming team: the Ethiopian Clowns. Pollock’s Clowns wore wigs and pancake makeup and played under faux-African pseudonyms such as Abbadaba and Tarzan. They also trafficked in humor that was, at best, minstrel-adjacent.
paragraphs.zh: 在20世纪1930年代创建食人族巨人队的人是一位前黑人联盟投手。该队在东北地区的订场负责人是一位来自纽约、具有杂耍喜剧（杂技滑稽）背景的经纪人。他叫西德·波洛克，后来因拥有另一支巡回表演球队“埃塞俄比亚小丑队”而闻名。波洛克的小丑队成员戴假发、涂厚重的浅色粉底，使用“阿巴巴巴”“泰山”等伪装成非洲风格的艺名。他们的表演也包含了一种顶多算是接近歌舞杂剧传统的喜剧内容。

paragraphs.en: Richard “King Tut” King, who’d previously had a stint with the Cannibal Giants, was the team’s most prominent comedian. He would emerge from the dugout looking like an escaped prisoner, or pantomime shooting craps before dropping his pants. His main collaborator on the Clowns was a dwarf known as Spec Bebop. (His real name was Ralph Bell.) In one recurring skit, a dentist gag, Tut would place a lit firecracker in Bebop’s mouth.
paragraphs.zh: 理查德“图特国王”金（Richard “King Tut” King）此前曾在食人族巨人队效力，是该队最著名的喜剧担当。他常常从临时休息区走出来，装扮成刚越狱的囚犯，或做一段哑剧式的掷骰子把戏，接着脱下裤子。小丑队中他最常合作的搭档是一名身材矮小的表演者，绰号“斯佩克·贝波”。（他本名叫拉尔夫·贝尔。）在反复上演的牙医小品里，图特会把一枚点燃的鞭炮放进贝波的嘴里。

paragraphs.en: In the early 1940s, Negro Leagues owners banded together to try to prohibit their teams from playing Pollock’s franchise. The ban proved impossible to enforce, though, because the Clowns were so popular—with both white and Black fans—that cash-poor Black-owned clubs felt they had no choice but to book them.
paragraphs.zh: 1940年代初，黑人联盟的球队老板们联合起来，试图禁止他们的球队与波洛克的球队交手。然而这项禁令始终难以执行，因为小丑队同时受到白人和黑人球迷的欢迎，经济拮据的黑人控股俱乐部在现实压力下只得把他们请来上场。

paragraphs.en: The Negro American League eventually eased up on its Clowns ban, admitting them to the league in 1943. The Clowns, for their part, promised to lose the makeup and stop calling themselves Ethiopian. The team, which had been nominally based in Miami, rebranded itself, becoming the Cincinnati Clowns before moving to Indianapolis. They got rid of the paint, too, though not right away. At Howard University’s Moorland-Spingarn Research Center, I found a 1945 program touting the Clowns as the “Most Sensational Ball Club in the World.” Next to that caption was a photo of 15 men with white clown makeup slathered on their faces.
paragraphs.zh: 黑人美洲联盟后来放松了对小丑队的禁令，于 1943 年正式接纳了他们。小丑队方面也承诺停止涂抹舞台妆，并停止自称“埃塞俄比亚人”。这支最初名义上驻扎在迈阿密的球队随即改头换面，先更名为辛辛那提小丑队，随后又迁往印第安纳波利斯。他们也停掉了油彩妆容，但并非立刻。就在霍华德大学摩尔兰-斯宾加恩研究中心，我看到一份 1945 年的节目单，把小丑队宣传为“世界上最令人瞠目结舌的棒球队”。在那句标语旁边，是一张照片，照片里有 15 名男子，脸上涂满了白色小丑妆。

paragraphs.en: The Clowns were at times competitive in their new league, but the NAL—and Black baseball as a whole—was soon decimated by integration. When Major League Baseball plucked away the likes of Jackie Robinson, Larry Doby, and Roy Campanella in the late 1940s, Black fans abandoned the Negro Leagues en masse.
paragraphs.zh: 小丑队在新联赛中有时也具有竞争力，但黑人美洲联盟以及整个黑人棒球很快就被职业融合进程重创。到了 20 世纪 40 年代后期，当大联盟棒球把杰基·罗宾逊、拉里·多比和罗伊·坎帕内拉等人“吸走”后，黑人球迷便成群地离开了黑人联盟。

paragraphs.en: Richard “King Tut” King, the team’s most prominent comic act, and his main collaborator, Ralph Bell, known as Spec Bebop (Dean Conger / The Denver Post / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 理查德“廷-塔特”（“King Tut”）·金与其主要搭档拉尔夫·贝尔（绰号“Spec Bebop”）（Dean Conger / The Denver Post / Getty）

paragraphs.en: The Clowns were better positioned than their peers to navigate the Negro Leagues’ collapse. That was largely because Pollock had a knack for making his team the center of attention. In 1953, the Clowns’ owner signed Toni Stone, declaring that baseball was the “latest masculine enterprise to fall before the advance of wearers of skirts and panties.” The team’s new female second baseman was a media and box-office sensation, reviving interest in the entire Negro American League. “Give the fans something they want to see,” a triumphant Pollock said, “and they’ll come out.”
paragraphs.zh: 小丑队比同业更能在黑人联盟崩解中站稳脚跟，这主要是因为波洛克擅长把自己的球队推到公众视线的中心。1953 年，小丑队老板签下了托妮·斯通，并宣称棒球是“最迟到现代的男性事业，却在女装者与穿紧身短裤的女性兴起之际屈服”。这位新晋的女性二垒手立刻成为媒体与票房热点，带动了整个黑人美洲联盟的关注热度。波洛克得意地说：“给观众他们想看的东西，他们就会来。”

paragraphs.en: The boom didn’t last. In his book Negro League Baseball: The Rise and Ruin of a Black Institution, the historian Neil Lanctot writes that crowds dwindled the next season despite the Clowns’ addition of two new women, Connie Morgan and Mamie “Peanut” Johnson. After the 1954 season, the Clowns left the Negro American League, and a year later, all three female pioneers were out of baseball. As an independent barnstorming team, the Clowns returned to their slapstick roots and played to smaller audiences. One game in 1964—estimated attendance: 400 people—featured the 4-foot-5 Billy Vaughn playing third base in a dress and the first baseman James “Natureboy” Williams pulling an oar, a golf club, a mannequin leg, and four baseball bats out of his pants.
paragraphs.zh: 热潮并未持续。历史学家尼尔·兰科特在《黑人联盟棒球：黑人机构的崛起与衰落》中写道，尽管小丑队（Clowns）新增了两位女性球员，康妮·摩根（Connie Morgan）和梅米“花生”约翰逊（Mamie “Peanut” Johnson），下一赛季观众仍在减少。1954赛季结束后，小丛丑队离开了黑人美国联盟，之后一年，三位女性先驱者都离开了棒球。作为一支独立的巡回表演队，小丑队回归到滑稽夸张的表演根基，并在更小的观众场次中比赛。1964年有一场比赛，估计到场人数只有400人，场上出现了身高4英尺5英寸的比利·沃恩（Billy Vaughn）身穿连衣裙在三垒手位上守备，和一垒手詹姆斯“Natureboy”威廉姆斯（James “Natureboy” Williams）从裤裆里掏出一只桨、一支高尔夫球杆、一条人体模型假腿和四根棒球棒。

paragraphs.en: In 1968, the Clowns reverse-integrated, adding their first white player. By the 1980s, the decade when the Clowns finally petered out, the team’s roster was entirely white.
paragraphs.zh: 到1968年，小丑队实现了“反向融合”，加入了他们的第一位白人球员。到了1980年代，也就是小丑队最终消失的十年，球队名单已经全部是白人球员。

paragraphs.en: A little after 9:30 a.m., three and a half hours before the first pitch of the new Indianapolis Clowns’ Sunday matinee in Memphis, Jackie Bradley Jr. was already on the field. His task that morning in early March was to lock down his part in a mid-game song-and-dance routine. When Outkast’s “Hey Ya!” started booming from the stadium PA, though, he seemed uncertain of his moves. My scouting report: For a dancer, he’s a great center fielder.
paragraphs.zh: 在上午9点半过后不久，也就是新印第安纳波利斯小丑队（Indianapolis Clowns）周日下午场在孟菲斯开打前三个半小时，杰基·布拉德利二世（Jackie Bradley Jr.）已经在球场上了。那年三月的清晨，他的任务是完成中场一套歌舞节目的一部分。可是，当奥特卡斯特（Outkast）的《Hey Ya!》从体育场的公共广播里响起时，他似乎有些拿不准动作。我对他做的观察是：这个“舞者”其实是个出色的中外野手。

paragraphs.en: The former Red Sox star, now 36, was the first ex–major leaguer to move to Banana Ball full-time. In his Clowns debut, he caught a fly ball behind his back. “I’ve had to rewire my brain to not just catch the ball,” he told me. “Any ball that’s hit to me, I almost have to do a trick play, because they just see me catch it normal, it’s like, Oh, whatever. Do something cool.”
paragraphs.zh: 这位现年36岁、曾效力红袜的前球星，是首位转向“香蕉球”全职模式的前大联盟球员。在他为小丑队首秀中，他在身后接住了一记高飞球。“我得把脑子重新‘改线’了，不能只想着把球接住，”他对我说，“任何打到我身上的球，我几乎都得做个花式动作，因为大家看到我正常接球就会想‘哦，随便了’——我得做点更酷的事。”

paragraphs.en: Mastery of trick-play theory and technique is a job requirement in Banana Ball. “These guys are baseball players,” Errick Fox, the team’s head coach, told me. “You also have to flip that switch and be like, Hey guys, you’re performers.”
paragraphs.zh: 在Banana Ball中，精通诈哨战术和技巧是一项工作要求。 “这些家伙是棒球运动员，”Bananas球队主教练Errick Fox告诉我，“你还得把开关一拧，变成‘嘿，伙计们，你们是表演者’。”

paragraphs.en: Fox grew up in Atlanta in the ’90s, when the Braves’ lineup featured Black superstars such as Fred McGriff, David Justice, and Deion Sanders. “At that time, it was exciting,” he recalled. “All of us kind of gravitated to really want to be like those guys.” One of his first responsibilities as the Clowns’ coach, he said, was to “get some Black ballplayers.”
paragraphs.zh: Fox在1990年代的亚特兰大长大，那时亚特兰大勇士队的打线里有Fred McGriff、David Justice和Deion Sanders等黑人超级明星。 “那时，真的很激动，”他回忆道。 “我们当时都有一种共鸣：都很想成为像他们那样的人。” 他表示，自己刚接任小丑队（Clowns）教练时的首要任务之一就是“招到一些黑人球员”。

paragraphs.en: Bradley, who’d made a cameo appearance with the Bananas in 2025 at the urging of his oldest daughter, was the team’s top recruit. The Clowns’ partnership with the Negro Leagues museum played a role in his decision to sign on, as did the potential to sway more Black athletes to get into baseball. As a young prospect in Virginia, he’d gotten used to being the only Black player on the field. “I enjoyed the people that I was around,” he said, “but I definitely wanted there to be more people to look like me.”
paragraphs.zh: Bradley因2025年在老二女儿的催促下在Bananas短暂亮相，之后成为球队的头号招募目标。小丑队与黑人联盟博物馆的合作关系也促使了他签约，此外还有机会吸引更多黑人运动员走入棒球事业这一考虑。作为一名在弗吉尼亚成长的年轻新秀，他早已习惯了自己在场上往往是唯一的黑人球员。 “我很喜欢身边的人，”他说，“但我确实希望场上能有更多看起来和我一样的人。”

paragraphs.en: Jackie Bradley Jr. was the first former major leaguer to play Banana Ball full-time. He was a star center fielder for the Red Sox; now he’s honing his juggling skills. (Kevin Wurm for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: Jackie Bradley Jr.是第一位全职参加Banana Ball的前大联盟球员。他曾是红袜队的王牌中外野手，如今他正在打磨自己的杂耍技巧。（The Atlantic 的Kevin Wurm）

paragraphs.en: Some of Bradley’s new teammates are Banana Ball veterans. Malachi Mitchell told me that his baseball philosophy is to “play loud”—to run hard, display his emotions, and try to put on a show. That approach didn’t always go over well in Florida’s youth-baseball scene. He ran across “a lot of racist people,” he said, and “the words they would use are arrogant or he’s doing too much.” For the Savannah Bananas, too much was the perfect amount. Mitchell adopted the persona of “Flash Tha Kid,” the Bananas’ designated runner, delighting fans with his speed on the basepaths, simulated banjo playing, and willingness to eat a disgusting amount of fettuccine Alfredo in service of a comedy bit. But as one of the organization’s few Black players, Mitchell told me he sometimes “felt a little separated.” When Jesse Cole asked him if he wanted to switch to the Clowns, he said yes right away. “Now I feel at home,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 布拉德利的一些新队友是“香蕉球”老将。马拉奇·米切尔（Malachi Mitchell）告诉我，他的棒球理念是“尽量夸张地打球”——全力奔跑、把情绪展现出来，并尽量把比赛变成一场表演。这种打法在佛罗里达的青少年棒球圈并不总是受欢迎。他说自己遇到“很多种族主义者”，而“他们会说一些傲慢的话，说他做得太过了”。对萨凡纳香蕉队来说，“太过”反而正好恰到好处。米切尔接下了“Flash Tha Kid”的角色，成为香蕉队指定的“快跑员”，他在跑垒上以速度、模拟弹班卓琴，以及为一段喜剧桥段而愿意吃掉大量恶心的奶油烩宽面条，赢得了观众欢呼。但作为球队少数的黑人球员之一，米切尔告诉我，他有时“会有点被孤立的感觉”。当杰西·科尔问他是否愿意转投小丑队时，他立刻答应了。“现在我有了归属感，”他说。

paragraphs.en: Another group of Clowns got pulled from baseball’s discard pile. When Nick Wilson threw his final pitch in college, he marked what he thought was the end of his career by tossing his cleats over a telephone wire. Last fall, he was selling computer equipment when he heard about a Banana Ball tryout in Nashville. It was the same audition where Kobe Robinson showed up in a Frozone costume. Wilson went as a Ninja Turtle.
paragraphs.zh: 另一支小丑队阵容也从棒球“弃置区”里被重新带回。尼克·威尔逊（Nick Wilson）在大学里投出了他最后一球后，以为职业生涯结束了，他把球鞋扔过电话线。去年秋天，他在卖电脑设备时听说了纳什维尔的一次“香蕉球”试训。那次试训也是科比·罗宾逊（Kobe Robinson）穿着弗罗兹（Frozone）装扮出现的同一场选拔赛。威尔逊当时则扮成一只忍者神龟。

paragraphs.en: Wilson told me his mother hadn’t been sure about the whole Clowns thing—she worried that the team’s name might put him in a bad light. But the franchise’s connection to the Negro Leagues was meaningful to him. Wilson’s grandfather James, who died in 2017, played pro baseball in Houston in the early ’50s, when sports were still segregated in the city. Now he had the chance to carry that legacy forward. “This is history,” he told me. “Why would I not want to be a part of a team called the Clowns?”
paragraphs.zh: 威尔逊告诉我，他母亲起初并不十分认可“加入小丑队”这件事——她担心球队名字会让他形象受损。但这支球队与黑人联盟的联系对他来说意义重大。威尔逊的祖父詹姆斯（James）在2017年去世，他曾在上世纪50年代初在休斯敦打职业棒球，那时这座城市的体育界仍然是种族隔离的。如今，威尔逊终于有机会把这段传承延续下去。“这是历史，”他说。“为什么我不想成为一支叫做小丑队的球队的一员呢？”

paragraphs.en: The team I saw in March wasn’t quite complete. A month later, the Clowns brought on Mo’ne Davis, who in 2014 became the first girl to throw a shutout in the Little League World Series. Twelve years later, she’s joined the Clowns’ lineage of pioneering Black female athletes. In Davis’s inaugural Clowns appearance, she retired her first batter on a routine grounder to third.
paragraphs.zh: 我在三月看到的这支球队还不算完全“齐整”。一个月后，小丑队又加入了莫妮·戴维斯（Mo’ne Davis）。她在2014年成为第一个在小联盟世界大赛中投出零封比赛的女孩。十二年后，她加入了小丑队这条开创黑人女性运动员先河的血脉。戴维斯在小丑队的首场亮相中，以一个常规的三垒游击内野飘球接杀，成功“退场”了她的第一个击球手。

paragraphs.en: From the April 2025 issue: Kaitlyn Tiffany explores why most women are not allowed to play baseball
paragraphs.zh: 来自2025年4月期：Kaitlyn Tiffany 探讨了为什么大多数女性不被允许打棒球

paragraphs.en: The new Clowns aren’t all Black. Among the team’s white players are the two most clownish guys on the roster: 20-year-old Fisher Polydoroff, a multi-instrumentalist magician who’s adopted the persona of a 1950s pitcher named “Ole Knuckles,” and 38-year-old Mat Wolf, a second-generation rodeo clown with a deep repertoire of trick pitches and methods for dropping his pants. (“You can do it quickly, you can do it slowly, you can do it with a spin,” he explained to me.)
paragraphs.zh: 新的“小丑队”并不全是黑人。队里的白人球员中，最会卖弄滑稽的两个人是20岁的Fisher Polydoroff和38岁的Mat Wolf。Polydoroff 是一位会弹多种乐器的魔术师，他塑造了一个名为“老奥莱·克纳克勒斯”（“Ole Knuckles”）的1950年代投手形象；Wolf 则是二代牛仔杂技小丑，精通各种花式投球和脱裤子的招式。（“你可以快速来一套、慢慢来一套、也可以转一转来，”他向我解释道。）

paragraphs.en: Saturday night’s game began with a ceremonial weigh-in. Wilson, the Clowns’ contestant, faced off against the Party Animals’ Jake Lialios, a man who (judging by his social-media presence) spends most of his waking hours covered in baby oil. Both players stood atop a pink scale, then flexed and danced bare-chested. Around the time when an on-field announcer described Wilson as “over six feet of meat,” it dawned on me that AutoZone Park would not be hosting a nuanced colloquy on Black representation in baseball.
paragraphs.zh: 周六晚上的比赛以一场“称重”仪式开始。小丑队的参赛者 Wilson 与派对动物队的 Jake Lialios 对阵。后者从他的社交媒体足以看出，平日醒着的大部分时间都是满身婴儿油。他们俩站在一台粉色体重秤上，然后上身裸露地甩胳膊、跳舞。当场边解说员称 Wilson “是个六英尺以上的壮硕人物”时，我才意识到，AutoZone体育场并不打算在这里上演一场关于棒球中黑人代表性的细腻讨论。

paragraphs.en: The Indianapolis Clown Nick Wilson during a weigh-in before a game against the Party Animals in March (Kevin Wurm for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 印第安纳波利斯小丑队Nick Wilson在与派对动物队对阵前的称重场景（照片摄于三月，作者Kevin Wurm，刊于《大西洋月刊》）

paragraphs.en: This weekend series was really about the Party Animals. They were the “home team” in Memphis, which meant the Clowns mostly got subsumed within their opponent’s hot-pink-and-glistening-pecs aesthetic. “As the away team, we just don’t have the same number of opportunities to share and kind of story-tell,” Joe Meyer told me.
paragraphs.zh: 这周末的系列赛真正的焦点其实是“派对动物”队。在孟菲斯，他们是“主场队”，这意味着小丑队大多被融入了对手那种荧光粉色加闪亮胸肌的整体视觉风格里。“作为客场队，我们就没有那么多机会去展示，也没有那么多机会去讲故事，”乔·迈耶告诉我。

paragraphs.en: Meyer is the Clowns’ 24-year-old show coordinator—the man responsible for developing the team’s look and feel. “In my role, I’ll say quite frankly, I think in some ways we had expected—or seriously considered—a person of color for it, and it’s something that’s important to us, and it is something to talk about,” he said. Nevertheless, Meyer, who is white, has the assignment, and he feels the significance of what he’s being asked to do: “Ultimately, how we build this brand is going to be how a lot of people remember not just the Clowns, but remember the Negro Leagues.”
paragraphs.zh: 迈耶是小丑队24岁的演出统筹，也就是负责打造球队整体形象和氛围的人。“以我的角色来说，我坦白说，在某种程度上我们曾经预期过——或者认真考虑过——让一个有色人种来担任这个职位，这对我们来说很重要，而且这是件值得讨论的事，”他表示。尽管如此，身为白人的迈耶仍接下了这个任务，而且他也意识到自己被要求去做的事情有多么重要：“最终，我们如何打造这个品牌，将决定很多人不仅记住小丑队，也记住黑人联赛（Negro Leagues）。”

paragraphs.en: Day to day, Meyer produces and directs the Clowns’ in-game entertainment. I first spotted him prowling the field with a clipboard, watching over the team’s “Hey Ya!” dance routine. That kind of choreographed sequence—what’s known in Bananaland as an “over-the-top moment,” designed to pop live in the stadium and later on social media—tends to be a relatively small-scale production. The project that’s really consuming Meyer is the Clowns’ home-team show, which is scheduled to debut in Indianapolis in May. That will be the time, he said, when “the full brand kind of comes to life.”
paragraphs.zh: 在日常工作中，迈耶负责制作和指导小丑队的赛中娱乐节目。我第一次见到他时，他拿着夹板在场地上来回巡视，监督球队的“Hey Ya!”舞蹈。那种编排好的舞段——在香蕉王国（Bananaland）里被称为“过度夸张时刻”，目的是在球场现场和后续社交媒体上都能“炸场”——通常规模都不算大。迈耶真正全心投入的项目，是小丑队的主场秀，计划于今年5月在印第安纳波利斯首演。迈耶说，那将是“品牌的完整面貌真正鲜明起势”的时刻。

paragraphs.en: Traditionally, Banana Ball branding hasn’t been difficult to parse. The Party Animals are party animals. The Firefighters fight fires. The Loco Beach Coconuts, this season’s other new team, are represented by a coconut wearing sunglasses.
paragraphs.zh: 传统上，香蕉球（Banana Ball）的品牌定义并不难懂。派对动物（Party Animals）本身就是派对动物。消防队（Firefighters）就是对抗火灾。洛科海滩椰子队（Loco Beach Coconuts）是这个赛季的另一支新球队，他们的形象则是一个戴着太阳镜的椰子。

paragraphs.en: The Clowns are more complicated. When Meyer started diving into the franchise’s past, he discovered a photo of the Ethiopian Clowns from their 1930s face-painting era. “You’re like, Whoa,” he said. “The Clowns name represented something a lot different than we think it did.”
paragraphs.zh: 这支球队的故事更为复杂。梅耶在深入研究该球会历史时，发现了一张他们在1930年代有面部彩绘时期“埃塞俄比亚小丑队”的照片。“你会觉得，天哪，”他说道，“这个‘小丑’这个名字当时代表的是我们现在想象中的完全不一样的东西。”

paragraphs.en: Early on, when Meyer and his colleagues were kicking around concepts for the team, he worried that they might be veering into whoa territory. He told me that he wrote up a five-page memo, essentially laying out the difference between a “minstrel show and the brand of today.” His takeaway: “If we’re super heavy in making these guys look like clowns, then we’re really playing into this just negative part of the history.”
paragraphs.zh: 一开始，梅耶和同事们在为球队构思概念时，他就担心自己可能走入了危险地带。他告诉我，他写了一份五页备忘录，核心就是梳理“早年的黑脸表演”和“当下品牌”的区别。他的结论是：“如果我们把这群人弄得过分像小丑，那我们其实是在利用这段历史里非常负面的一面。”

paragraphs.en: The Clowns’ logo, a pair of big red shoes, nods to the circus without approaching the realm of wigs and face paint. Still, a team known as the Clowns that can’t look too clownish presents a marketing challenge. So does the mandate to educate as well as entertain. “Fans come to Banana Ball games expecting the greatest show in sports, and a show that is more than just a Negro Leagues history night,” Meyer said.
paragraphs.zh: 小丑队的标志是一双红色的大鞋子，向马戏团致意，却并未触及假发和面部彩绘的领域。尽管如此，一个叫“小丑”的球队却又不能太像小丑，这本身就是个营销难题。更何况球队还肩负着既要娱乐又要教育的使命。梅耶说：“球迷来香蕉球场看球时，期待的是体育界最精彩的演出，而不仅仅是一次黑人联赛历史之夜。”

paragraphs.en: In Memphis, the best preview of a fully realized Indianapolis Clowns experience came during pregame introductions. That segment began with some musical theater: a song, “Here Come the Clowns,” that borrows its tune and pop-history approach from Hamilton’s “My Shot.” Written and performed by the team’s hosts, Brandon Bomer and Jarius Jones—both young Black men—it provides some useful background for fans who don’t know the team’s history (“We started in the Negro League, when it was hard, sweat, tears, and blood we bleed”).
paragraphs.zh: 在孟菲斯，印第安纳波利斯小丑队体验的完整前序演出，最好的前奏，是在赛前介绍环节。那一段从音乐剧式开场开始：一首名为《Here Come the Clowns》（小丑来啦）的歌曲，旋律和流行史处理方式都借鉴了《Hamilton》里的《My Shot》。“该队的两位主持人布兰登·博默（Brandon Bomer）和贾里乌斯·琼斯（Jarius Jones）都很年轻，且都是黑人男性演唱和创作这首歌”，这对不熟悉球队历史的观众提供了有用背景：“我们起步于黑人联赛，那时艰难，我们流过汗、流过泪，也流过血。”

paragraphs.en: The Clowns do a pregame dance. (Kevin Wurm for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 小丑队先来一段赛前舞蹈。（Kevin Wurm，载于《大西洋月刊》）

paragraphs.en: When that musical number was over, more than a dozen players picked up baseballs and started juggling—for a juggler, Jackie Bradley Jr. is also a great center fielder—while Fisher Polydoroff and Mat Wolf threw pitches back and forth between their legs. The team then started playing shadow ball, with choreography inspired by the original Clowns’ routine. There was a dive for an invisible grounder, an invisible throw from deep in the hole, and an invisible pop-up that bonked someone on the head. At one point, everyone started moving in extreme slow motion, before speeding up again. Then the finale: Malachi Mitchell did a backflip, caught an invisible ball at home plate, and—like magic—pulled a real ball from his glove.
paragraphs.zh: 当那段音乐舞蹈结束后，十几个球员捡起棒球开始玩玩杂耍——要说起花样球员，杰基·布拉德利二世（Jackie Bradley Jr.）也是个出色的中外野手——而菲舍·波利多罗夫（Fisher Polydoroff）和马特·沃尔夫（Mat Wolf）则在两条腿间抛接球。接着球队开始表演“影球”，动作编排受到了原版小丑队套路的启发。有人假装扑向看不见的滚地球，有人假装从死球区深处接一个看不见的传球，还有人接一个“无形”高飞球，结果把人砸了一下脑袋。某一刻，全队齐刷刷地进入极慢镜头般的动作，然后又加快了速度。最后的收尾是：马拉奇·米切尔（Malachi Mitchell）做了个后空翻，在本垒板处接住了一个看不见的球，然后——像变魔术一样——从手套里掏出了一颗真球。

paragraphs.en: Before the Clowns’ Sunday game in Memphis, I started hearing rumors about a special guest—a figure from the team’s past. Around 11:30 a.m., he showed up at the ballpark: a small, thin man with a slight smile, dressed in a navy Notre Dame hoodie and a vintage baseball cap embroidered with a red C.
paragraphs.zh: 在小丑队周日于孟菲斯的比赛前，我就开始听到关于一位特邀嘉宾的传闻——一位来自球队过去的身影。大约上午11点半，他出现在球场：一个个头不高、身材瘦削、带着淡淡微笑的男人，穿着一件海军蓝的圣母大学（Notre Dame）连帽衫，戴着一顶绣着红色“C”的复古棒球帽。

paragraphs.en: Reginald Howard first crossed paths with the Clowns in the late 1940s, when he worked for the team as a batboy near his hometown of South Bend, Indiana. A decade later, he became their second baseman. Now, at 91 years old, he sat in the dugout at AutoZone Park as the modern-day Clowns leaned in close.
paragraphs.zh: 里贾纳德·霍华德（Reginald Howard）在20世纪40年代末与小丑队首次相遇，那时他在印第安纳州南本德（South Bend，靠近他的家乡）为球队当打球小童。十年后，他成为了球队的二垒手。如今，91岁的他坐在AutoZone Park的替补席上，观看今日版的小丑队在现场一一接近。

paragraphs.en: Howard told them that he was a “disciple” of Rube Foster, the Black baseball legend who founded the first sustained Negro League. He talked about long bus rides and asked if they’d heard of the two-way star Martín Dihigo. (Kobe Robinson said yes—Dihigo is a playable character in the video game MLB: The Show.) Then Nick Wilson, the pitcher whose grandfather played in segregated Texas, asked Howard a question: How can we get Black kids to come back to baseball? His answer: You’ve got to get them while they’re young.
paragraphs.zh: 霍华德告诉他们，他是“鲁布·福斯特”的追随者。福斯特是创立第一支持续运作的黑人联盟（Negro Leagues）的黑人棒球传奇。霍华德谈到长途巴士乘车，并问他们是否听说过两栖球星马丁·迪西戈。（科比·鲁滨逊说听说过——迪西戈是电子游戏《MLB：The Show》中的可玩角色。）随后，投手尼克·威尔逊——他祖父曾在德州实行种族隔离时踢球——向霍华德提了一个问题：我们如何让黑人孩子重新回到棒球场？霍华德回答：你得趁他们还小的时候把他们吸引过来。

paragraphs.en: Howard had recently published a book about how baseball lost Black children in the first place. When he stepped onto the field to get saluted by the sold-out crowd, the PA announcer read the title in a cheery vibrato: Baseball’s Silent Genocide. I don’t have comprehensive records, but I believe this was the first time the word genocide was uttered over a loudspeaker at a Banana Ball game.
paragraphs.zh: 霍华德最近出版过一本关于棒球最初为何失去黑人孩子的书。当他走上球场接受满座球迷的致敬时，场馆广播员用轻快的颤音报出了书名：〈棒球的沉默种族灭绝〉。我没有完整的统计，但我相信这是第一次在香蕉球（Banana Ball）比赛上用扩音器喊出“种族灭绝”这个词。

paragraphs.en: At a Starbucks the next day, Howard handed me a copy of his book and laid out his thesis. He believes there was a “sordid conspiracy” to preserve baseball as a predominantly white sport. As far back as his childhood, Black kids were told—and many came to believe—“this malarkey” that they were better equipped for sports other than baseball. He also watched resources shift toward all-white suburbs while they vanished in America’s inner cities. Many of the Black players who did stick with baseball ended up profoundly isolated. In the worst case, he wrote, “you begin to think there’s something wrong with you” just for liking the game.
paragraphs.zh: 第二天在星巴克，霍华德递给我一本他的著作，并讲述了自己的论点。他认为，有一场“丑闻式的阴谋”在努力把棒球维持为一项以白人主导的运动。追溯到他童年时，黑人孩子就被告知——而且许多人也因此相信——“这套说法”认为他们更适合其他运动，而不是棒球。他还看到资源正向全部白人居住的郊区转移，而在美国城市内部则不断凋零。许多坚持留在棒球界的黑人球员最终变得非常孤立。最糟糕的情况下，他写道，仅仅因为喜欢这项运动，人也会“开始以为自己有问题”。

paragraphs.en: The number of Black players in Major League Baseball rose for decades after integration. But that growth eventually stalled, then reversed. The historian Louis Moore has shown that at baseball’s lower levels, Black talent was dwindling as far back as the early 1970s. Moore doesn’t call this a conspiracy, but he does attribute the decline in part to informal racial quotas.
paragraphs.zh: 在大联盟实现融合后的几十年里，黑人球员人数曾持续上升。但这种增长最终停滞，随后又转为下降。历史学家路易斯·摩尔（Louis Moore）指出，从1970年代初起，在棒球基层，黑人天赋就已在不断减少。摩尔并不把这称为阴谋，但他确实把这一衰退部分归因于非正式的种族配额。

paragraphs.en: Major League Baseball now has a variety of initiatives to make the sport more accessible to Black athletes: youth academies, inner-city programs, showcases for players from historically Black colleges. The league has seen some positive returns; nine of the first 21 picks in the 2024 MLB draft were Black, and the overall percentage of Black Americans in the majors has increased slightly in the past two years. An MLB spokesperson told me that nearly a third of the 64 Black players on Opening Day rosters in 2026 emerged from the league’s development pipeline.
paragraphs.zh: 美国职业棒球大联盟（MLB）目前已推出多项举措，让这项运动对黑人运动员更具可达性：青年学院、城市社区青训项目，以及面向历史性黑人大学球员的展示会。该联盟已看到一些积极回报；2024届MLB选秀前21号签中有9人是黑人，而过去两年，大联盟中黑人美国人占比也略有上升。一位MLB发言人告诉我，2026年开幕日名单上的64名黑人球员中，几乎三分之一是从联盟的人才培养通道中走出来的。

paragraphs.en: Reginald Howard, who is 91, played for the original Indianapolis Clowns in the 1950s. In March, he visited with the new Clowns. (Kevin Wurm for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 91岁的雷金纳德·霍华德在20世纪50年代为原来的印第安纳波利斯小丑队（Indianapolis Clowns）效力。3月，他走访了新的小丑队。（摄影：Kevin Wurm，刊载于《大西洋月刊》）

paragraphs.en: Jesse Cole and Bob Kendrick are, in their way, contributing to the mission to revive Black baseball. But the problem with bringing back the Clowns is the same problem the team posed in its heyday. For as long as the Negro Leagues existed, Black ballplayers and their champions were battling for recognition and respect. What they were fighting against was the idea that their version of the game was nothing but a clown show.
paragraphs.zh: 杰西·科尔（Jesse Cole）和鲍勃·肯德里克（Bob Kendrick）从某种意义上也在为复兴黑人棒球的事业贡献力量。但重建小丑队面临的问题，与这支球队在鼎盛时期曾面临的问题是同一个。只要黑人联盟存在，黑人球员及其倡导者就一直在为争取认可和尊重而奋斗。他们反对的正是这样一种观念：他们的棒球形式不过是一场小丑表演。

paragraphs.en: It started with The Pittsburgh Courier’s Wendell Smith, a forceful advocate for integration in the 1940s, who believed that the Ethiopian Clowns risked discrediting the entire Negro Leagues. It continued with Piper Davis, who mentored Willie Mays as a player and manager for the Birmingham Black Barons. “If you were Black, you was a clown. Because in the movies, the only time you saw a Black man, he was a comedian or a butler,” Davis once said, reflecting on life and baseball under Jim Crow. “But didn’t nobody clown in our league but the Indianapolis Clowns. We played baseball.”
paragraphs.zh: 这场斗争始于20世纪40年代的《匹兹堡快报》记者温德尔·史密斯（Wendell Smith），他是一位大力推动种族融合的倡导者，认为“埃塞俄比亚小丑队”有可能让整个黑人联盟失去信誉。后来又由皮珀·戴维斯（Piper Davis）接续，戴维斯曾在伯明翰黑男爵队（Birmingham Black Barons）担任球员和经理并指导威利·梅斯（Willie Mays）。他曾回忆道：“如果你是黑人，那你就是个小丑。因为在电影里，你看到的黑人往往只有两个角色：喜剧角色或男管家。”这反映了他在吉姆·克劳（Jim Crow）体制下的生活和棒球经历。“但我们联盟里，除了印第安纳波利斯小丑队，没有哪个人是小丑。我们是打棒球的。”

paragraphs.en: Historians of Black baseball have long struggled with how to tell the story of the Clowns. Larry Lester, one of the co-founders of the Negro Leagues Baseball Museum, told me he saw the old Clowns play as a child in the 1960s. “The skits were hilarious,” he said. “But as a kid, I didn’t realize the negative stereotypes that were being portrayed.” Now, when he recounts the history of the Negro Leagues, he focuses on the greatest Black baseball teams ever assembled, such as the Homestead Grays and the Pittsburgh Crawfords.
paragraphs.zh: 黑人棒球历史学者长期以来一直在思考如何讲述“小丑队”(Clowns)的故事。黑人棒球博物馆的共同创始人之一拉里·莱斯特（Larry Lester）告诉我，他在20世纪60年代还是孩子时就看过“老克劳恩斯”上场比赛。“那些小品非常搞笑，”他回忆道，“但当时作为一个孩子，我没有意识到其中呈现出的负面刻板印象。”如今，当他讲述黑人棒球历史时，重点放在了有史以来最强的黑人棒球队伍，如霍姆斯蒂德·格雷斯（Homestead Grays）和匹兹堡克劳福德队（Pittsburgh Crawfords）等。

paragraphs.en: Another of the museum’s co-founders, Phil Dixon, told me that he intentionally omitted King Tut and Spec Bebop from his acclaimed photographic history of the Negro Leagues: They weren’t the image of Black baseball that he wanted to portray.
paragraphs.zh: 博物馆另一位共同创始人菲尔·迪克森（Phil Dixon）告诉我，他在自己备受赞誉的黑人棒球摄影史著作中，刻意省略了“国王图特（King Tut）”和“斯佩克·贝博普（Spec Bebop）”这两人：“因为他想呈现的并不是他们所代表的那种黑人棒球形象。”

paragraphs.en: Lester and Dixon have, through their research and advocacy, helped get Black baseball legends enshrined in Cooperstown and have their statistics acknowledged in the sport’s official record books. But despite their best efforts, the Clowns still play an outsize role in public memory.
paragraphs.zh: 莱斯特和迪克森通过研究和倡议，帮助多位黑人棒球传奇球员入选了库珀斯敦（Cooperstown）名人堂，并使他们的统计数据被纳入这项运动的官方纪录册。尽管他们做出了最大努力，黑衣小丑队（Clowns）仍在公众记忆中占据着远超其实际影响力的位置。

paragraphs.en: Malachi “Flash Tha Kid” Mitchell. His baseball philosophy is to “play loud”—which didn’t always go over well in Florida’s youth-baseball scene. (Kevin Wurm for The Atlantic)
paragraphs.zh: 马拉奇·“闪电侠”·米切尔（Malachi “Flash Tha Kid” Mitchell）。他的棒球理念是“放得响亮”—但这在佛罗里达州青少年棒球圈里并不总是受欢迎。（凯文·沃姆（Kevin Wurm），《大西洋月刊》）

paragraphs.en: Kendrick, who presides over the Negro Leagues museum today, told me that he does not want to hide the uglier elements of the Clowns’ history. At the museum, a display featuring the Clowns notes that they “strengthened common black stereotypes and were frowned upon by league management and the black press.” Below the sign: a photo of King Tut in a billowing clown suit.
paragraphs.zh: 凯德里克，如今负责经营黑人联赛博物馆的人，告诉我他不想掩盖小丑队历史中较为丑陋的一面。在博物馆里，有一处展示专门介绍小丑队，说明他们“强化了常见的黑人刻板印象，并且受到联盟管理层和黑人新闻界的反对”。在这段说明牌下方，摆放着一张金图图身穿飘逸小丑服的照片。

paragraphs.en: How do you append that kind of caveat to the show that the new Clowns are putting on? I wanted to ask Jesse Cole this and other questions. But the Banana Ball PR team, which made the Clowns’ players available to me in Memphis, stopped replying to my inquiries and never granted my requests to speak with Cole. Meyer, the show coordinator, also didn’t respond after initially promising to share his five-page memo on what separates the modern-day Clowns from the minstrelsy of their ancestors.
paragraphs.zh: 那我应该如何把这种警示语附加到新小丑队正在表演的节目上呢？我想向杰西·科尔提这类问题以及其他问题。可为我在孟菲斯提供小丑队球员采访的香蕉球新闻团队不再回复我的咨询，也始终没有批准我与科尔会面的请求。节目统筹梅耶（Meyer）在最初承诺要和我分享他写的五页备忘录，说明如今的小丑队与其先祖所从事的“黑脸表演”有什么不同后，也未再回应。

paragraphs.en: In our conversation, Meyer had emphasized that the larger story he wants to tell about the Clowns is one of triumph and unity—how a group of Negro Leagues players overcame adversity on the way to success. “This is all about us coming together, and it doesn’t matter who you are, doesn’t matter what you look like; you can be part of this team,” he said. The uplifting narrative he was laying out didn’t much resemble the scholarship of Lester and Dixon, to say nothing of Reginald Howard’s jeremiad.
paragraphs.zh: 在我们的谈话中，梅耶强调他想讲述的小丑队主线故事，是一段关于胜利与团结的故事——一群黑人联赛球员如何克服逆境走向成功。“这完全是关于我们共同奋斗的事，而且你是谁、你长什么样并不重要；你都可以成为这支球队的一员，”他说。这个激励人心的叙事，与莱斯特和迪克森的学术研究并不太接近，更不用说雷金纳德·霍华德那篇长篇抨击式文章了。

paragraphs.en: “Yes, race is a part of it,” Meyer said. “But how do we make the story about the individual players and who the Negro Leagues and the original Indianapolis Clowns actually were?” He added, “I think that that story is so, so much bigger than the story of segregation.”
paragraphs.zh: 梅耶说：“是的，种族是其中的一部分。” “但我们要如何把故事聚焦在个人球员身上，以及黑人联赛和原始印第安纳波尔斯（印第安纳小丑队）到底是什么样的球队？”他补充道：“我认为，这个故事远远大于‘种族隔离’这个故事。”

paragraphs.en: Who were the original Indianapolis Clowns? Some of them were history makers, like Hank Aaron and Toni Stone. Others, like King Tut and Spec Bebop, were entertainers. But most were just ordinary baseball players, men like Howard.
paragraphs.zh: 最初的印第安纳波利斯小丑队（Indianapolis Clowns）到底是谁组成的？其中有人是历史人物，比如汉克·阿隆（Hank Aaron）和托尼·斯通（Toni Stone）；还有像“国王图特”与“斯佩克·贝博普”这样的表演者。但大多数人只是普通的棒球运动员，像霍华德那样的人。

paragraphs.en: In Memphis, Howard was introduced on the field as a man who’d suited up for the Clowns “as they barnstormed across the country, bringing joy and world-class baseball to fans of all backgrounds.” But that wasn’t the baseball life he’d actually lived. In his day, the late ’50s, the Clowns didn’t typically draw big crowds, and they weren’t playing at a world-class level. “I would like to say that I played in the same league as Larry Doby and Jackie Robinson, but it wasn’t,” he told me. “The caliber of ball was not the same.”
paragraphs.zh: 在孟菲斯，霍华德当时被介绍为曾穿上小丑队球衣，在全国各地巡回表演（barnstorm）的人，“为各类背景的球迷带去欢乐和世界级的棒球”。但那并不是他真正经历过的棒球生活。在他那个年代——五十年代后期——小丑队通常并不吸引大批观众，也不是以世界级水平上场的。他对我说：“我本来想说我是在和拉里·多比（Larry Doby）及杰基·罗宾逊（Jackie Robinson）同一联盟打球，但实际上不是。”他接着说：“比赛水准并不一样。”

paragraphs.en: Howard said it’s always bothered him that so much storytelling about Black baseball is “rhetoric and lies.” Given the relative dearth of information about the game’s preintegration stars, the history of the Negro Leagues has long been full of tall tales—Josh Gibson hitting a home run so prodigious that the ball didn’t come down until the next day. In the midst of all this mythology, Howard believes, telling a true story can be a radical act.
paragraphs.zh: 霍华德说，关于黑人棒球的许多叙述一直困扰着他，因为都充满“修辞和谎言”。由于关于隔离前明星球员资料相对匮乏，黑人联盟的历史长期以来都充斥着夸张的传闻，比如乔什·吉布森（Josh Gibson）据说轰出一支主场落点要到第二天才落地的全场最强本垒打。在这层层神话之中，霍华德认为，讲述一段真实的故事本身就是一种激进行为。

paragraphs.en: When I asked Howard to describe himself as a player, he said that he was only “fair,” with quick feet, quick hands, and a good mind, but also a weak arm and warning-track power. I couldn’t help but think of Kobe Robinson and Nick Wilson, talented young players who didn’t quite have what it takes to make it all the way to the majors. Now they have the chance to keep playing the game they love.
paragraphs.zh: 当我请霍华德自我评价时，他说自己只是“勉强及格”，有速度快的脚、敏捷的手和不错的判断力，但手臂不算强，击球也缺乏边线外的飞球杀伤力（warning-track power）。我不禁想起科比·罗宾逊（Kobe Robinson）和尼克·威尔逊（Nick Wilson），这两位天赋出众的年轻球员差点就差那么一点，没能一路打进大联盟。如今，他们终于有机会继续打自己热爱的这项运动。

paragraphs.en: From the July/August 2023 issue: Mark Leibovich on baseball’s desperate effort to save itself from irrelevance
paragraphs.zh: 刊于2023年7月/8月号：《马克·莱博维奇：棒球如何奋力摆脱被边缘化的危险》

paragraphs.en: Whether the new Indianapolis Clowns succeed in bringing Black fans and athletes back to baseball may come down more to players like Robinson and Wilson than to Bob Kendrick and Jesse Cole. In Memphis, I watched the Clowns sign autographs for a predominantly white crowd. I also saw Wilson lean down to speak with a group of Black children, giving them time and attention and a different image of what a baseball player could look like. He was getting them while they’re young.
paragraphs.zh: 印第安纳波利斯小丑队能否把黑人球迷和黑人运动员重新吸引回棒球，可能更多取决于像罗宾逊和威尔逊这样的球员，而不是鲍勃·肯德里克与杰西·科尔。在孟菲斯，我看到小丑队给以白人观众为主的群体签名。后来我也看到威尔逊走过去与一群黑人孩子交谈，耐心地给他们时间和关注，给他们展示了一个完全不同的“棒球员长什么样”。他是在抓住他们还小的时候做这件事。
